hypothesis: B-EVAL3: external grounding ratio < 5%. Human signals mostly self-directive, not external validation.
method: Classified 118 human signals in HUMAN-SIGNALS.md by type (external vs directive vs quality-concern). Counted 19 challenge entries with external evidence. Measured PHIL-16 session-based target.
note: B-EVAL3 confirmed. External grounding at 5.0-5.1% matches the hypothesis exactly. Binding constraint: PHIL-16 session-based target (1/10 sessions with external validation) = 40 required, 6 actual (15% of target). Additionally, 0/19 challenge entries cite external evidence — all self-referential.
next: F-EVAL2 baseline established. Next action: F-COMP1 (competitions domain) is the only viable external grounding path. Retest at S420 (after ~10 sessions of F-COMP1 advancement).
